zh.lifeSummary: 當Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford於大約1066年出生在英國素福爾可克萊爾時，他的父親Richard FitzGilbert de Clare37歲，母親Rohese Giffard33歲。他於1085年與Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont在英國賀特福爾賀特福德結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於1117年11月17日在英國維爾爾文特爾布恩 蒙可頓享年52歲，埋葬在英國肯特托布利其。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford于大约1066年出生在英国素福爾可克萊爾时，他的父亲Richard FitzGilbert de Clare37岁，母亲Rohese Giffard33岁。他于1085年与Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont在英国賀特福爾賀特福德结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他去世于1117年11月17日在英国維爾爾文特爾布恩 蒙可頓享年52岁，埋葬在英国肯特托布利其。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Clare
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Clare, Wicklow, and Wexford): habitational name from Clare in Suffolk (an eastern county of England), the center of a major Norman baronial estate since the Norman Conquest. The English placename probably originated as a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright, gentle, or warm’. The surname is not from the Irish county name Clare, which is either from the surname or from a place called with Gaelic clár ‘plank bridge’. Another Norman surname in the southeast of Ireland was de Clere, from Clères in Seine-Maritime (France), named from the local river.

English and French: from the Middle English, Old French female personal name Cla(i)re (from Latin Clara, from clarus ‘clear, bright, famous’) which achieved some popularity, greater elsewhere in Europe than in England, through the fame of Saint Clare of Assisi (see Chiara ). The English surname seems to have been interchangeable with Clear 1.

English: occupational name from an agent derivative of Middle English cley ‘clay’. The work of medieval clayers involved plastering a framework of interwoven twigs with mud to produce wattle-and-daub work.

variants.name: Claire
meanings: Irish: variant of   Clare  .
Variant of   Clair  , an altered form of German   Klare  .
French: variant of   Clair  .

variants.name: Chiara
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Marco, Amedeo, Angelo, Gino, Rocco.

Italian: from the female personal name Chiara or Clara, a name popularized in the medieval period by the cult of Saint Clare of Assisi (c. 1193–1253); she was an associate of Saint Francis of Assisi and founded the order of nuns known as the Poor Clares. See also Chiaro .

variants.name: Claar
meanings: German: variant of Klaar (and, in North America, an altered form of this), a nickname from Middle High German, Middle Low German klar ‘pure, pretty, sensible’, or a habitational name from a minor place so named near Braunschweig.
Possibly also an Americanized form of Dutch Klaar: from a vernacular form of the female personal name Clara (see Clare 2).

variants.name: Clarkin
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): from a pet form of the personal name Clare (compare Clare and Clear ), which is both a masculine and a female personal name in Middle English.
Irish (Monaghan): variant of   Clerkin  .

variants.name: Clear
meanings: English (southeastern England, of Norman origin): from Clere, the French popular form of the personal name Clare 2, and possibly interchangeable with it or confused with it.

English (southeastern): probably a habitational name from clere, a component of several placenames in north Hampshire (Highclere, Burghclere, Kingsclere). This is probably from a Celtic stream name meaning ‘bright’ (cognate with Latin clarus ‘clear, bright’).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Klar   1.

nameMeanings.name: Gilbert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from gisil ‘pledge’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. This was the name of the founder of the only native British religious order (abolished at the Dissolution of the Monasteries), St Gilbert of Sempringham ( ?1083–1189 ), in whose honour it is still sometimes bestowed, especially among Roman Catholics. It gained a wider currency in the 19th century.

historicEvents.title: Battle of Hastings
historicEvents.description: Battle of Hastings was fought between the Norman-French army and the battle-wary English army on October 14, 1066. Duke William of Normandy believed he was the rightful king rather than Harold, who had been crowned king several months earlier. Harold was killed in battle and William was crowned king on Christmas Day 1066.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford was born about 1066, in Clare, Suffolk, England, his father, Richard FitzGilbert de Clare, was 37 and his mother, Rohese Giffard, was 33. He married Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont in 1085, in Hertford, Hertfordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 5 daughters. He died on 17 November 1117, in Winterbourne Monkton, Wiltshire, England, at the age of 52, and was buried in Tonbridge, Kent, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford est né environ 1066, à Clare, Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, Richard FitzGilbert de Clare, avait 37 ans et sa mère, Rohese Giffard, avait 33 ans. Il a épousé Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont en 1085, à Hertford, Hertfordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 5 filles. Il est décédé le 17 novembre 1117, à Winterbourne Monkton, Wiltshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 52 ans, et a été enterré à Tonbridge, Kent, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford um 1066 in Clare, Suffolk, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard FitzGilbert de Clare 37 und seine Mutter Rohese Giffard 33. Er hat 1085 in Hertford, Hertfordshire, England Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Er starb am 17. November 1117 im Alter von 52 Jahren in Winterbourne Monkton, Wiltshire, England und wurde in Tonbridge, Kent, England begraben.

Lui sposato Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont nel 1085, nel Hertford, Hertfordshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 5 figlie. ko.Tonbridge Castle, Tonbridge, Kent, Engla: 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 토브리지 토브리지 카스틀
ko.lifeSummary: Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford(이)가 대략 1066년 영국 수폴크 클래르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard FitzGilbert de Clare(은)는 37세였고, 그의 어머니 Rohese Giffard(은)는 33세였다. 그는 1085년 영국 허트퍼드실르 허트퍼드에서 Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1117년 11월 17일 영국 윌츠히르 윈터보우른 몬k턴에서 52세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 켄트 토브리지에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford nasceu aproximadamente 1066, em Clare, Suffolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard FitzGilbert de Clare, tinha 37 anos e sua mãe, Rohese Giffard, tinha 33 anos. Ele casou-se com Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont em 1085, em Hertford, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 5 filhas. Ele faleceu em 17 de novembro de 1117, em Winterbourne Monkton, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, com 52 anos, e foi sepultado em Tonbridge, Kent, Inglaterra.

ru.Tonbridge Castle, Tonbridge, Kent, Engla: тонбридж кастл, тонбридж, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford родился около 1066 г. в местности клар, суффолк, Англия, его отцу по имени Richard FitzGilbert de Clare было 37 лет, а матери, которую звали Rohese Giffard, 33. Он женился на женщине по имени Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont в 1085 г. в местности хиртфорд, хиртфордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Он умер 17 ноября 1117 г. в местности винтирбурн монктон, уилцхир, Англия в возрасте 52 лет и был похоронен в местности тонбридж, кэнт, Англия.

Se casó con Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont en 1085, en Hertford, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 17 de noviembre de 1117, en Winterbourne Monkton, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 52 años, y fue sepultado en Tonbridge, Kent, Inglaterra.
